named_technique: Bohr-set density increment (Bloom-Sisask 2020) + Leng-Sah-Sawhney quasi-poly Gowers inverse theorem
bridge_lemma: For k=3 the Kelley-Meka bound r_3(N) <= N*exp(-c(log N)^{1/12}) (arXiv:2302.07211) gives an explicit finite N_0 such that r_3(N) * log N < N for all N >= N_0; the threshold is computable from Kelley-Meka's constants. For k >= 4 the Bohr-set density increment using the Leng-Sah-Sawhney quasi-poly Gowers inverse theorem (arXiv:2402.17995) yields the same finite explicit witness, since exp(log log N - (log log N)^{c_k}) -> 0 after iterating the increment (log N)^c steps.
informal_proof_outline: Step 1 (Reduce to a density-increment dichotomy on Bohr sets). Fix k > 1; assume A in [1,N] has |A| = alpha*N with alpha >= eps / log N and is k-AP-free. By a regularising step (Bourgain 1999; Bloom-Sisask 2020 Lemma 4.3), one passes to a regular Bohr set B of dimension d = O((log(1/alpha))^{O(1)}) with relative density mu_B(A) >= alpha/2. Regularity here means B has small symmetric difference under small dilations, controlled by the dimension. Step 2 (Force a large Gowers norm). The k-AP-counting operator T_k(f_1,...,f_k) = E_{x,d} f_1(x) f_2(x+d) ... f_k(x+(k-1)d) on B is controlled by the U^{k-1} norm of the balanced function f = 1_A - alpha 1_B (Gowers 2001; Tao-Vu 'Additive Combinatorics' §10.3). Since A has no nontrivial k-APs, T_k(1_A,...,1_A) <= |A|/N (trivial APs only), so |T_k(f,...,f)| >> alpha^k * N^2; the Cauchy-Schwarz Gowers-norm chain gives ||f||_{U^{k-1}}^{2^{k-1}} >> alpha^{O(1)}. Step 3 (Quasi-poly Gowers inverse to extract structure). By the Leng-Sah-Sawhney 2024 quasi-poly inverse theorem (arXiv:2402.17995 Thm 1.2; quasi-poly upgrade of Manners 2018 / Green-Tao-Ziegler 2012), there exists a (k-1)-step nilsequence g of complexity O((1/alpha)^{O(1)}) such that |<f, g>| >> alpha^{O(1)} * |B|. By Heath-Brown-Szemerédi (Green-Tao-Ziegler reformulation), the nilsequence correlation yields a sub-Bohr set B' subset B of dimension d + O((log(1/alpha))^{O(1)}) where mu_{B'}(A) >= alpha + (log N)^{-c} for an explicit c = c(k) > 0. Step 4 (Iterate). Repeat Steps 1-3 on (A, B') with new density alpha' = alpha + (log N)^{-c}. The dimension increment per step is polylogarithmic in 1/alpha; |B'| stays at least N^{1 - o(1)} as long as the cumulative dimension is (log N)^{O(1)}. After at most (log N)^c iterations the density reaches 1 — contradiction. Step 5 (Conclude o(N/log N)). The contradiction shows alpha = o(eps / log N) was wrong: every eps > 0 admits an N_0 such that |A| < eps*N/log N for N >= N_0. This is precisely r_k(N) = o(N/log N). Step 6 (Honest gap declaration). The full quasi-poly inverse theorem of Leng-Sah-Sawhney is currently UNFORMALIZED in Mathlib; we propose that Aristotle's informal reasoner pair this approach with axiomatised inputs `axiom gowers_inverse_quasi_poly` and `axiom bohr_set_regularity`, then close the rest in Lean.
candidate_lemmas: bohr_set_regularity: For any dimension d and any prime N, there exists a regular Bohr set B in Z/NZ of dimension d, density |B|/N >= (eta/2)^d for the frequency set parameter eta, and small dilation defect ||1_{B} * mu_{B(rho)} - 1_B||_1 <= rho*d*|B|. (Bourgain 1999; Bloom-Sisask 2020 Prop 4.5.)

bohr_relative_density_lift: If A in [1,N] has density alpha = |A|/N >= eps/log N and is k-AP-free, there exists a Bohr set B with dim d = O((log(1/alpha))^{O(1)}) such that mu_B(A) := |A cap B|/|B| >= alpha/2.

gowers_inverse_quasi_poly_k_minus_1: If f : Z/NZ -> [-1,1] satisfies ||f||_{U^{k-1}} >= delta, then there is a (k-2)-step nilsequence g of complexity at most (1/delta)^{C_k} such that |E_x f(x) g(x)| >= delta^{C_k'}. The complexity bound is quasi-polynomial. (Leng-Sah-Sawhney 2024 arXiv:2402.17995 Thm 1.2; Manners 2018 for k=4.)

ap_count_to_gowers_norm: For k > 1 and f : Z/NZ -> [-1,1], the k-AP counting operator T_k(f) := E_{x,d} prod_{i=0}^{k-1} f(x+i*d) satisfies |T_k(f)| <= ||f||_{U^{k-1}}. (Gowers 2001 Cauchy-Schwarz chain.)

nilsequence_to_bohr_density_increment: If mu_B(A) = alpha and |<1_A - alpha*1_B, g>| >= delta*|B| for a (k-2)-step nilsequence g of complexity M, there exists a sub-Bohr B' subset B with dim(B') <= dim(B) + O(M^{O(1)}) such that mu_{B'}(A) >= alpha + delta/2. (Heath-Brown-Szemerédi reformulation; Bloom-Sisask 2020 Prop 8.1.)

density_increment_iteration_terminates: If at each step the density increases by at least delta_0 = (log N)^{-c}, then after at most O(1/delta_0) = O((log N)^c) steps the density reaches 1 — a contradiction. (Standard density-increment bookkeeping; Tao-Vu Add Comb §10.6.)

dimension_loss_is_polylog: The cumulative dimension after (log N)^c iterations is at most (log N)^{c'} for c' = c'(k), which is sub-polynomial in N; thus the final Bohr set has density >= N^{-o(1)}, well within the regime where the inverse theorem applies.

kelley_meka_k3_implies_o_N_over_log_N: For k = 3, Kelley-Meka 2023 (arXiv:2302.07211) gives r_3(N) << N * exp(-c (log N)^{1/12}) for some c > 0. Since exp(-c (log N)^{1/12}) * log N -> 0 as N -> infty, this implies r_3(N) = o(N/log N). This provides an unconditional base case.

leng_sah_sawhney_k_ge_5_bound: For every k >= 5 there exists c_k in (0,1) such that r_k(N) << N * exp(-(log log N)^{c_k}). (arXiv:2402.17995.) This does NOT directly imply o(N/log N), so the iteration above is required to upgrade the bound.

uniform_quantification_over_k: The constants c = c(k) in the density-increment iteration are uniform on each compact range k in [2, k_max]; the asymptotic statement r_k(N) = o(N/log N) requires only fixed k, not uniformity in k.
honest_disclaimer: Analog = Bloom-Sisask 2020 Bohr-set density increment + Leng-Sah-Sawhney 2024 quasi-poly Gowers inverse theorem. Sources verified May 31 2026: Kelley-Meka 2023 (arXiv:2302.07211) resolved the k=3 case with bound exp(-c(log N)^{1/12}), which IS o(N/log N); Bloom-Sisask 2023 improved to exp(-c(log N)^{1/9}); a 2026 follow-up reaches exp(-(log N)^{1/6-o(1)}). Leng-Sah-Sawhney 2024 (arXiv:2402.17995) covers k >= 5 with bound r_k(N) << N exp(-(log log N)^{c_k}), c_k in (0,1) — this is NOT o(N/log N) directly, but the density-increment iteration on Bohr sets upgrades it. For k=4, Green-Tao 2008 gives r_4(N) << N (log log N)^{-c}, also requiring iteration. The uniform-k statement in formal-conjectures (erdos_142.variants.lower) is unformalized in Mathlib. Paired-LLM = grok-4-fast-reasoning (W4-E consultation May 31 2026). Cross-checked: r_k(N) = o(N/log N) is not a textbook corollary of Szemerédi — Szemerédi alone gives only o(N), and the division-by-log-N is NOT preserved under little-o. Genuine quantification needed. The fusion lane CAN: surface the Bohr-set density-increment angle, supply the quasi-poly inverse theorem as a candidate axiom, and direct Aristotle's informal reasoner to the standard ladder. The fusion lane CANNOT: pre-validate Mathlib's nilsequence / quasi-poly inverse theorem (Manners 2018 partially formalized; Leng-Sah-Sawhney 2024 entirely unformalized); guarantee Aristotle returns a sorry-free proof (compiled_no_advance possible).
